3.happening: Remember the first day's price as the cheapest buy price seen so far.
3.why: We need a baseline buy price before any profit can be measured.
3.invariant: min_price is the lowest price among the days seen so far.

4.happening: Start the best profit at 0 — no trade beats doing nothing.
4.why: If prices only fall, the answer must stay 0 (we simply don't trade).
4.invariant: max_profit is the best profit achievable up to now.

5.happening: Advance to the next day to consider selling on.
5.why: Every day is a potential sell day; we scan them left to right.
5.invariant: All days before i have already been considered.

6.happening: Look at today's price so we can test selling now.
6.why: Selling today only pays off against the cheapest earlier day.
6.invariant: i marks today; price will hold prices[i].

7.happening: Is today's price ({price}) below the cheapest seen ({min_price})?
7.why: A new low means a cheaper day we could have bought on.
7.invariant: If yes, today becomes the new buy candidate.

8.happening: Today is cheaper — record it as the new lowest buy price.
8.why: Buying at the lowest possible price maximizes every later profit.

9.happening: Profit of buying at the cheapest ({min_price}) and selling today ({price}) is {price - min_price}.
9.why: This is the best profit any sell-today plan can produce.
9.invariant: profit is today's best achievable gain.

10.happening: Is today's profit ({price - min_price}) better than our best ({max_profit})?
10.why: We keep only the single most profitable transaction.

11.happening: New record — lock in this profit as the best so far.
11.why: It beats every earlier transaction we tried.
11.invariant: max_profit is the largest profit found up to today.

12.happening: All days scanned — return the best profit ({max_profit}).
12.why: One pass sufficed because we only ever needed the cheapest earlier price.
12.invariant: max_profit is the global best; 0 if no profitable trade existed.

init.happening: Setting the baseline: cheapest price and zero profit.
init.why: We need a buy price and a starting best before scanning.

loop.happening: Scanning the days one at a time as sell candidates.
loop.why: Each day is a chance to sell against the cheapest earlier day.
loop.invariant: Days left of the cursor are done.

check.happening: Checking whether today is a cheaper day to have bought.
check.why: A lower buy price raises every future profit.
check.invariant: min_price stays the lowest price seen.

update.happening: Measuring today's profit and keeping it if it's a new best.
update.why: Only the single best transaction matters.
update.invariant: max_profit tracks the best profit so far.

return.why: Achieved in one pass with O(1) memory.
return.invariant: max_profit is the answer (0 if no profit was possible).
